REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS ANTHONY GO, RAYMOND GO, AND QUAN YUNG GO REVOKABLE TRUST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTY SHIFTED ITS BURDEN ON WHETHER STIPULATION IN THE PRIOR ACTION BARS THE CURRENT ACTION. OPPOSITION FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WITH COMPETENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. OPPOSITION PAPERS FILED ON NOVEMBER 22, 2022, ARE STRICKEN. ONLY OPPOSITION PAPERS FILED ON NOVEMBER 4, 2022 ARE CONSIDERED. MOTION IS DENIED TO THE EXTENT IT IS BASED ON (1) MALPRACTICE BEING A DEFENSE TO ENFORCEMENT OF A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT - MSJ IS FRAMED BY THE PLEADINGS (COMPLAINT AND ANSWER), THE ISSUE OF MALPRACTICE DOES NOT APPEAR IN ANY PLEADINGS; AND (2) TERMINATING SANCTIONS FOR VIOLATION OF A COURT ORDER - MOVING PARTY FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT MAY BE USED TO ADDRESS DISCOVERY TERMINATING SANCTIONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) AND CCP SECTION 437C(G). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Joseph D. O'Neil; Tarkington, O'Neill, Barrack & Chong, APC; 707-576-1380; appearing for defendants Anthony Go, Raymond Go, and Quan Yung Go Revocable Trust.
Ari E. Moss; Moss Bollinger LLP; 310-982-2984; appearing for plaintiff Ying Wang.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. Defendants contest the denied portions of the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ANTHONY GO, RAYMOND GO, AND QUAN YUNG GO REVOKABLE TRUST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden on whether Stipulation in the prior action bars the current action. Opposition failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence. Opposition papers filed on November 22, 2022, are stricken. Only opposition papers filed on November 4, 2022 are considered. Motion is DENIED to the extent it is based on (1) malpractice being a defense to enforcement of a settlement agreement - MSJ is framed by the pleadings (complaint and answer), the issue of malpractice does not appear in any pleadings; and (2) terminating sanctions for violation of a Court order - moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Motion for Summary Judgment may be used to address discovery terminating sanctions.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b) and CCP Section 437c(g). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
